message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/decorators.py,src/click/exceptions.py,src/click/parser.py
note: Implementing dynamic default resolution for options (dict-based defaults keyed by other option names). Need to own core.py (Option class, Command.main resolution pass) and types.py. Let me know if your feature also needs these files so we can split more precisely.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Adding batch_size parameter to multiple options (Option class) for chunked processing. Need to edit Option class logic in core.py and possibly type conversion in types.py. Please tell me which files/areas you need so we can split non-overlapping regions if we share files.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: We both need core.py so full file-disjointness isn't possible, but our features touch different regions: mine is dict-based dynamic defaults -> Option.__init__ default-type handling, a new Option.resolve_dynamic_default()-style method, and a second-pass loop I'll add in Command.main() (called via BaseCommand.main/invoke). Yours is batch_size -> likely Option.__init__ new kwarg plus processing in Parameter/Command.invoke or wherever batching happens. Proposal: I'll only touch Option.get_default/default-related code and add a small hook in Command.main(); please avoid those spots and add your batch_size code in separate methods/params. I'll also take types.py fully (need it for dynamic-default type validation) - do you actually need to edit types.py, or just core.py? Let's each finish our edits, then diff-review before finalizing to make sure we didn't collide.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py (Option class only, lines ~2465-3036: __init__, to_info_dict, and a new batch-iteration method)
your_files: src/click/core.py (everything else: Parameter base class, Context, Command, Group, Argument), src/click/types.py (all)
note: My batch_size feature is fully containable within the Option class body in core.py - I don't need to touch Parameter base class methods (get_default, type_cast_value on Parameter, etc.) or types.py at all. I'll add an override inside Option rather than editing the shared Parameter.type_cast_value. This should keep us disjoint even though we're both in core.py. Let me know if that works, or if your dynamic-default logic needs to live inside Option's __init__ specifically.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py (Option class: __init__ new batch_size kwarg + its own validation block, to_info_dict, plus a new override method e.g. type_cast_value/process_iter added within Option only)
your_files: src/click/core.py (Parameter base class, Option.get_default, Command.main/invoke, everything else), src/click/types.py (all)
note: Agreed - I don't need types.py at all, it's yours fully. In core.py I will only: add a new 'batch_size' kwarg to Option.__init__ (separate param from your default-handling changes, added near 'count'/'multiple' kwargs), add its own __debug__ validation (batch_size requires multiple=True, ValueError if negative), extend Option.to_info_dict, and add a new method inside the Option class to yield batches - I will NOT touch Option.get_default, Parameter base class methods, or Command.main/invoke. Will ping you with DONE + diff when finished so we can cross-check for collisions before finalizing.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Good news: I don't actually need Option.__init__ or to_info_dict at all - dynamic defaults just reuse the existing self.default attribute (already set generically by Parameter.__init__), no new stored state needed. So let's split Option class by METHOD instead of by file: YOU keep __init__ (lines ~2533-2664), to_info_dict (~2665-2677), and your new batch-iteration method (please add it right after to_info_dict, around line 2678, before get_error_hint). I will only touch: get_help_extra (~2822-2900, adding a dict-default branch next to the existing 'elif inspect.isfunction(default_value)' dynamic-default display line) and get_default (~2912-2926, so a dict default returns None here instead of leaking the raw dict as the value). I'll also add one new method of my own right after get_default (~2927) for resolving the dynamic value, plus edit Parameter.__init__ (~2076-2170, base class, you said you don't need this) for validating dict-default shape, plus Command.main (~1295-1408) for the second-pass resolution loop. This should keep our diffs on non-overlapping line ranges even within the same file/class. Does this work for you?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py: Option.__init__ (~2533-2664), Option.to_info_dict (~2665-2677), new batch-iteration method placed right after to_info_dict (~2678, before get_error_hint)
your_files: src/click/core.py: Parameter.__init__ (~2076-2170), Option.get_help_extra (~2822-2900), Option.get_default (~2912-2926), your new dynamic-default resolution method (~2927+), Command.main (~1295-1408); src/click/types.py (all)
note: Confirmed, this split works. Proceeding to implement now within my agreed regions only. Will send DONE with a diff summary when finished.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py: Parameter.__init__ (~2076-2170), Option.get_help_extra (~2822-2900), Option.get_default (~2912-2926), new dynamic-default resolution method (~2927+), Command.main (~1295-1408); src/click/types.py (all)
your_files: src/click/core.py: Option.__init__ (~2533-2664), Option.to_info_dict (~2665-2677), new batch-iteration method after to_info_dict
note: Confirmed, proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py: Option.__init__ (batch_size kwarg + validation), Option.to_info_dict, new Option.type_cast_value override
your_files: src/click/core.py: Parameter.__init__, Option.get_help_extra, Option.get_default, your new method, Command.main; src/click/types.py (all)
note: Implementation finished and tested (manual CLI invocation + full existing test suite except test_info_dict.py, which needs its hardcoded expected dicts updated for the new batch_size key - that's an expected/intentional change per spec, not a regression). My diff only touches lines 2500-2706ish in core.py within the Option class, matching our agreed split. Ready to merge whenever you are.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py: Parameter.__init__, Option.get_help_extra, Option.get_default, Option.resolve_dynamic_default, Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults, Command.main; src/click/types.py: convert_type
note: Implementation finished, all 739 existing tests pass, manually verified all spec examples (basic dict default, multi-dependency with boolean flags, envvar integration, help text showing [default: (dynamic)], TypeError validation for bad keys/values, no-match fallback to None). Stayed within our agreed regions, did not touch Option.__init__/to_info_dict. Ready to finalize.
